articles.title: A Single Neuron Is Sufficient to Bypass Safety Alignment in Large Language Models
articles.snippet: Safety alignment in language models operates through two mechanistically distinct systems: refusal neurons that gate whether harmful knowledge is expressed, and concept neurons that encode the harmful knowledge itself.
